primary_diagnosis.name: Acute panic attack (cancer-fear) triggered by incidental reticular oral lichen planus; airway patent

pathophysiology_summary: Reticular oral lichen planus produces bilateral, symmetric, painless white lacy streaks (Wickham striae) on the buccal mucosa, usually asymptomatic and often found incidentally at dental checks. It is benign; the reticular form in particular carries very low malignant potential and frequently needs no treatment. Suddenly noticing the lesions and reading about oral cancer triggered a panic attack with palpitations, hyperventilation and a subjective sensation of throat closure — but airway patency is demonstrable over video (normal voice, full sentences, swallowing water, no stridor). The emergencies to exclude are true airway compromise/anaphylaxis (absent) and a suspicious oral lesion (a solitary indurated ulcer/plaque — not the picture here).

differential.diagnosis: Panic attack with incidental reticular oral lichen planus
differential.evidence_basis: Panic triggered by noticing benign lacy oral lesions, with a patent airway.
Screen for stridor, aphonia, lip/tongue swelling; absent here.
A solitary indurated plaque/ulcer would concern for malignancy; the bilateral lacy pattern is not that.
Excluded — the streaks do not scrape off and the patient is asymptomatic.

differential.diagnosis: Panic attack with incidental reticular oral lichen planus
differential.evidence_basis: Patent airway (DI-001), bilateral painless non-scrapable lacy streaks without ulcer/induration (DI-002), panic onset on noticing/googling (DI-003) — benign oral lichen planus with a panic attack.
Normal voice/swallow, no stridor or angioedema — airway patent.
Bilateral symmetric lacy pattern without induration/ulcer is not malignancy; routine confirmation suffices.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: reassure (airway patent, benign lesion, not cancer), coach breathing, stop compulsive checking, routine dental/GP confirmation, and an airway safety-net. ED/ambulance for a patent airway is over-triage.

red_flags.description: True airway compromise/anaphylaxis — stridor, aphonia, lip/tongue swelling, inability to swallow liquids
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed airway emergency is rapidly fatal.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Escalating a patent airway to ED, or reinforcing the cancer fear, instead of reassuring with the demonstrable findings.
Reassuring without checking that the lesion is the benign bilateral lacy pattern and not a solitary indurated ulcer/plaque.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Formal oral examination and, if needed, biopsy of any atypical area
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Reassure using demonstrable airway/benign findings; routine dental/GP confirmation and baseline photography; airway safety-net.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Over-triaging a patent airway wastes resources and amplifies anxiety; failing to check the lesion pattern could miss a suspicious lesion.
